vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely obsolete. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/unidentifiable. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not visible as distinct specimens. P3 (Pothos) is the only identifiable biological entity, though it is in a state of terminal decline.

plant_audit.P3_Pothos: The specimen has undergone rapid senescence. The primary leaf, which was turgid in the earliest image, has undergone chlorosis and is now exhibiting severe necrotic wilting and postural collapse.
plant_audit.Soil_Surface: Presence of crushed eggshell fragments observed across the soil surface, likely an amateur intervention for calcium supplementation, now acting as a moisture-wicking barrier.

biome_observations.soil_texture: The soil appears consistently dark and potentially waterlogged, with no visible signs of aeration or healthy root activity.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Debris (eggshell fragments) is scattered, indicating a lack of maintenance or physical disturbance.
biome_observations.anomalies: The white rabbit anchor has been removed or obscured in the final frames, suggesting a loss of the scale reference point.

temporal_deltas.Earliest_to_T-3: Initial stability; P3 leaf is upright.
temporal_deltas.T-3_to_T-1: Progressive drooping of the P3 leaf; loss of structural integrity.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_Current: Total collapse of the P3 leaf; the specimen is now effectively non-viable.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of total collapse. The Pothos (P3) is suffering from severe physiological stress, likely due to root rot or environmental neglect (lack of light/improper moisture). The absence of other registered plants suggests a complete failure of the indoor botanical system.
vision_report.anomalies: The disappearance of the white rabbit anchor is a significant anomaly, indicating a disruption in the monitoring environment.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a multi-stage audit of the provided image sequence. My first action was to map the current visual data against the provided registry, which revealed a near-total loss of the expected botanical specimens. I then validated this by tracking the degradation of the Pothos (P3) from the earliest frame to the current state, noting the transition from a turgid, healthy leaf to a necrotic, collapsed state. The presence of eggshells suggests an attempt at soil amendment that failed to prevent the systemic decline of the biome. The environment is currently non-viable.
